title: Chrysophyllum masoalense
taxonomy: Chrysophyllum masoalense is classified under the family Sapotaceae. This family includes other species of Sapotaceae, which are primarily tropical trees. Chrysophyllum masoalense itself is a species within the genus Chrysophyllum, which comprises approximately 25 species of tropical trees. The Sapotaceae family is a part of the order Ebenales, and the class Magnoliopsida. Chrysophyllum masoalense is also known to be a part of the kingdom Plantae.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections.
